title: Space Technology Engineer
description: A Space Technology Engineer in India designs and develops spacecraft, satellites, and other space-related systems. They work on projects like launch vehicle development, payload design, and mission planning. Their day involves collaborating with teams, conducting research, and testing prototypes to ensure they meet the required standards.
entrance_exams: JEE Main, JEE Advanced, ISRO SET
degree_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Aerospace Engineering
top_govt_colleges: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay, Indian Institute of Technology Madras, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur
top_private_colleges: Vellore Institute of Technology, Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Manipal Academy of Higher Education, SRM Institute of Science and Technology

career_progression.year_0_2: Assist in design and development of spacecraft and satellites, work under supervision, and gain hands-on experience.
career_progression.year_3_5: Take on more responsibility, lead small teams, and contribute to project planning and execution.
career_progression.year_6_10: Hold senior positions, lead large teams, and oversee multiple projects.
senior_paths: Project Manager, Technical Lead, Research Scientist
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a company focused on space technology, leveraging expertise and network.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities exist in countries like the US, UK, and Australia, but require additional education and certifications.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Challenging to find internships and jobs, requires perseverance and networking.
common_misconceptions: Space Technology Engineers only work on government projects
It's a highly lucrative career with immediate high salaries
